entries.description: Read project-guidance.md, lessons-learned.md, session-set-authoring-guide.md, ai-led-session-workflow.md, and spec.md. User instructed router restricted to end-of-session verification only this session and to use Gemini Pro as the verifier.
Wrote session-state.json marking session 1 of set 003 in-progress (currentSession=1, lifecycleState=work_in_progress, orchestrator=claude-code/claude-opus-4-7@high, totalSessions=4).
Per durable user instruction (memory: feedback_ai_router_usage), routed ai-assignment.md authoring intentionally skipped; consistent with sessions 1-5 of set 001 and sessions 1-4 of set 002.
Confirmed Set 1 deliverables (queue_db.py, disposition.py, session_events.py) and Set 2 deliverables (mode-aware route()/verify(), enqueue path, role daemons) are present and importable.

Wrote ai-router/close_session.py: full CLI with all 9 documented flags (--session-set-dir, --json, --interactive, --force, --allow-empty-commit, --reason-file, --manual-verify, --repair, --apply, --timeout), CloseoutOutcome dataclass with stable JSON shape, exit-code table, idempotency via session-events.jsonl closed-state probe, disposition-required-or-force gate, closeout_requested/closeout_succeeded/closeout_failed event emission, gate-check skeleton with 5 named stub checks (working_tree_clean, pushed_to_remote, activity_log_entry, next_orchestrator_present, change_log_fresh), verification-wait skeleton (Session 3 fills polling), --repair skeleton walk (Session 4 fills detection logic).

Wrote ai-router/tests/test_close_session_skeleton.py with 22 unit tests covering: every documented flag accepted; --force/--interactive, --force/--manual-verify, --force/--repair, --apply-without-repair, and zero/negative timeout combinations rejected; missing session-set-dir → exit 2; missing disposition without --force → exit 2; --force bypass with no disposition → exit 0; happy-path skeleton → exit 0 with all 5 named gates passing; closeout_requested+closeout_succeeded events emitted and durable; idempotent no-op on already-closed set; --repair skeleton → exit 0; main() returns exit code; JSON output parseable; JSON shape stable on exit 2; RESULT_TO_EXIT_CODE table complete.
Routed end-of-session verification to gemini-pro (per user instruction: Gemini Pro). Cost $0.0283, in=13292t out=1172t. Saved raw output to session-reviews/session-001.md.

Round 1 verification by Gemini Pro returned ISSUES_FOUND with 3 issues (1 Major spec deviation, 2 Minor test gaps). All 3 fixed: --reason-file is now consumed and lands in closeout_requested event payload; closeout_failed emission test added via monkeypatch; missing-state fallback test added. Test count increased from 22 to 26; full suite 374 passed.
Routed round-2 verification to gemini-pro. Cost $0.0203, in=13780t out=310t. Appended raw output to session-reviews/session-001.md.

Read project-guidance.md, lessons-learned.md, ai-led-session-workflow.md, and spec.md. User instructed: no external provider routing this session except cross-provider verification routed to Gemini Pro.

Wrote session-state.json marking session 2 of set 003 in-progress (currentSession=2, lifecycleState=work_in_progress, orchestrator=claude-code/claude-opus-4-7@high). Appended work_started event to session-events.jsonl.
Per durable user instruction (memory: feedback_ai_router_usage), routed ai-assignment.md authoring intentionally skipped this session.

Created ai-router/gate_checks.py: 5 deterministic gate predicates with shared (passed, remediation) shape. check_working_tree_clean (scoped to session-set dir + files_changed allowlist + universal ignore patterns including .DS_Store/*.swp/.close_session.lock/session-events.jsonl, uses git status --porcelain -uall to defeat directory collapse). check_pushed_to_remote (enumerates detached HEAD, missing upstream, ahead-of-upstream, non-fast-forward, branch protection via git push --dry-run signal matching). check_activity_log_entry (verifies entry for currentSession exists). check_next_orchestrator_present (final-session shortcut; otherwise routes through validate_next_orchestrator). check_change_log_fresh (final-session-only; passes via mtime>=startedAt OR session-number reference in body). GATE_CHECKS registry preserves skeleton order.

Created ai-router/close_lock.py: file-based concurrency lock at <set>/.close_session.lock. acquire_lock uses O_CREAT|O_EXCL atomic create. Stale-lock reclaim via TTL (10 min) OR dead-PID detection. Cross-platform _pid_running: POSIX os.kill(pid, 0); Windows OpenProcess via ctypes with proper argtypes/restype and use_last_error=True for ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETER vs ERROR_ACCESS_DENIED disambiguation. release_lock is no-op when foreign PID holds the file. close_session_lock context manager.

Replaced _run_gate_checks stub in close_session.py to iterate GATE_CHECKS registry; predicate exceptions become failed-gate remediation rather than crashing the flow. Wrapped run() main path and repair branch in acquire_lock/release_lock; lock contention surfaces result=lock_contention exit code 3. Lock reclaim warnings flow into outcome.messages.

Wrote ai-router/tests/test_gate_checks.py: 24 tests. Helpers create real git repos with bare remotes. Coverage: clean tree pass, untracked-in-scope fail, universal pattern ignore (.DS_Store/.swp), out-of-scope filtering, not-a-git-repo fail; clean push pass, missing upstream, detached HEAD, ahead-of-upstream needs-push, divergent non-fast-forward; activity log present, missing state, missing log, no-matching-session; next_orchestrator final-skip, missing on non-final, malformed specifics, valid; change-log nonfinal-skip, missing on final, fresh-by-mtime, fresh-by-content-reference, stale-fail; registry order stability.

Wrote ai-router/tests/test_close_lock.py: 10 tests. Acquire creates file with PID/worker_id/timestamp, release removes file. Second acquire raises LockContention. Stale by TTL reclaimed with warning. Stale by dead PID reclaimed with warning. Foreign-PID release is no-op (does not remove peer file). Already-gone release is no-op. Context manager acquires/releases on success and on exception; propagates LockContention.

Wrote ai-router/tests/test_close_session_integration.py: 7 end-to-end tests against real git repo + real session-set fixtures. Real close-out succeeds. Concurrent peer triggers lock_contention exit 3. Stale lock from dead PID is reclaimed; close-out proceeds with warning in messages. Uncommitted in-scope file triggers gate_failed exit 1 naming working_tree_clean. Final session without change-log triggers gate_failed naming change_log_fresh. --force still bypasses gates but still acquires the lock; --force is blocked by a live peer.

Updated test_close_session_skeleton.py to monkeypatch _run_gate_checks in the started_with_disposition fixture and the session-zero test, since the skeleton tests were previously written against a stub-passes-everything implementation. Skeleton tests now isolate flow-control concerns from gate-check correctness; gate correctness lives in test_gate_checks.py.
Full pytest suite: 415 passed in ~30s on Windows (was 374 in Session 1 baseline; +41 net new tests).

Routed end-of-session verification to gemini-pro per user instruction (overrode session-verification task_type_override which defaults to gpt-5-4). Round 1: Gemini Pro returned substantive pass with all 6 acceptance criteria affirmed Met, but used non-standard verdict wording ("**Verdict:** pass") that the orchestrator-side parser does not recognize. Cost $0.0519, in=34416t out=888t. Saved to session-reviews/session-002.md.

Round 2 re-verify with explicit schema instruction. Router auto-escalated past Gemini to Opus because Gemini response was short (21 tokens output) and tripped the truncation/short-response escalation heuristic. Round 2 verdict: VERIFIED (single line + summary, no issues). Escalation surfaced an unintended Anthropic spend ($0.5434) on top of the user instruction restricting routing this session — flagging transparently. Cost $0.5434, in=36124t out=21t. Appended to session-reviews/session-002.md.

Added lessons-learned entry: "Schema-Only Re-Verifies Need max_tier Pinned To Block Auto-Escalation". Captures the round-2 re-verify failure mode where a wording-only re-verify against Gemini Pro escalated to Opus due to short-response heuristic, costing an unintended Anthropic call. Action: future schema-only re-verifies must pass max_tier=<verifier_tier>.
Marked session 2 complete in session-state.json with verdict=VERIFIED. Emitted verification_completed and work_verified events.

Read project-guidance.md, lessons-learned.md, ai-led-session-workflow.md, and spec.md. User instructed: no external provider routing this session except cross-provider verification routed to Gemini Pro.

Wrote session-state.json marking session 3 of set 003 in-progress (currentSession=3, lifecycleState=work_in_progress, orchestrator=claude-code/claude-opus-4-7@high). Appended work_started event to session-events.jsonl.
Per durable user instruction (memory: feedback_ai_router_usage), routed ai-assignment.md authoring intentionally skipped this session.

Implemented _wait_for_verifications in close_session.py: queue-mode polling against queue_db. Discovers per-provider queues under provider-queues base dir, polls every poll_interval_seconds (default 5s) until each verification_message_id reaches a TERMINAL state, capped by --timeout minutes. Returns (method, wait_outcome, message_ids, _MessageOutcome list). wait_outcome is completed/failed/timed_out; missing message ids in any provider queue are treated as failed (drift surface). Injectable sleep/monotonic let tests avoid wall-clock waits. Wired into run(): emits one verification_completed (or verification_timed_out) event per message with queue_provider/queue_state/failure_reason payloads. Outcome routing: timed_out -> verification_timeout exit 4 + closeout_failed event; failed/missing -> gate_failed exit 1 with synthetic verification_completed gate result. --force still short-circuits the wait; --manual-verify already short-circuited.

Wrote ai-router/reconciler.py: walks docs/session-sets, derives lifecycle state via current_lifecycle_state(read_events()), picks STRANDED_STATES (CLOSEOUT_PENDING/CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED) older than DEFAULT_QUIET_WINDOW_MINUTES=5, re-invokes close_session.run() per stranded set. ReconcileEntry/ReconcileSummary dataclasses; action enum: skipped_no_events/skipped_not_stranded/skipped_too_recent/rerun_succeeded/rerun_gate_failed/rerun_verification_timeout/rerun_lock_contention/rerun_other/error. Per-set runner exceptions caught -> action=error, sweep continues. register_sweeper_hook() builds the orchestrator-startup callable Set 6 will wire. CLI: python -m ai_router.reconciler with --base-dir/--quiet-window-minutes/--json. Default runner uses timeout=1 so the sweep stays responsive (real outage surfaces as rerun_verification_timeout next sweep).

Wrote ai-router/tests/test_close_session_verification_wait.py: 5 integration tests against real git repo + bare remote. Outsource-first (api method) skips queue wait (no verification_completed events emitted). Outsource-last happy path: fake_sleep drives queue forward (claim then complete) between poll cycles; close_session blocks until message terminates, then succeeds; verification_completed event emitted with queue_provider=gpt-5-4-mini. Timeout case: monotonic clock injected to jump past deadline; result=verification_timeout exit 4; verification_timed_out + closeout_failed events emitted. Verifier rejection: drove message to failed via 3x claim/fail; result=gate_failed; verification_completed gate names the failure. --manual-verify with pending message + sleep raising AssertionError confirms the wait is bypassed.

Wrote ai-router/tests/test_reconciler.py: 17 tests. Walk filters by spec.md presence; missing base_dir returns empty summary. work_started-only set is skipped_not_stranded; closeout_succeeded is skipped_not_stranded (closed); fresh-without-events is skipped_no_events; recently-stranded under quiet window is skipped_too_recent. Backdating last event timestamp 30m makes a stranded set eligible: rerun_succeeded path records close_session result+messages. Parametrized action mapping for noop_already_closed/gate_failed/verification_timeout/lock_contention/repair_drift. RuntimeError in runner is captured per-set without aborting the sweep. register_sweeper_hook callback receives ReconcileSummary. format_summary renders entries; CLI --json emits structured payload. End-to-end: real git repo + close_session runner recovers a stranded api-mode session (closeout_requested without closeout_succeeded backdated) -> rerun_succeeded, closeout_succeeded event lands.
Full pytest: 437 passed in ~35s on Windows (was 415 in Session 2 baseline; +22 net new tests: 5 verification-wait + 17 reconciler).

Routed end-of-session verification to gemini-pro per user instruction (overrode session-verification task_type_override which defaults to gpt-5-4; pinned max_tier=2 per lessons-learned to block auto-escalation). Round 1: ISSUES_FOUND with 3 Minor items (1 event-naming suggestion, 1 gate-check naming, 1 test gap). Cost $0.0425, in=27276t out=840t. Saved to session-reviews/session-003.md.

Handled round-1 findings: (1) rejected event-rename (verification_completed -> verification_failed) with documented rationale: EVENT_TYPES is a fixed Set-1 enum and verification_completed.docstring already says verdict lives in payload (queue_state=failed, failure_reason=...) — adding a new event type is out of scope. (2) Accepted gate-check rename: verification_completed -> verification_passed in close_session.py:1070; closeout_failed.failed_checks now reads ["verification_passed"]. (3) Accepted test gap fix: test_verifier_failed_terminates_with_gate_failed now asserts the per-message verification_completed event with queue_state=failed and ISSUES_FOUND in failure_reason, plus closeout_failed.failed_checks payload. All 55 close_session tests still green.

Routed round-2 re-verify to gemini-pro with max_tier=2 (no escalation triggered — short response heuristic stayed within Gemini per lessons-learned). Verdict: "ISSUES_FOUND: NO" with empty issues array — substantively VERIFIED, non-standard verdict wording. Confirms fixes are correct and item-1 rejection is sound. Cost $0.0234, in=17299t out=177t. Appended to session-reviews/session-003.md.
Marked session 3 complete in session-state.json with verdict=VERIFIED. Emitted verification_completed and work_verified events.

Read project-guidance.md, lessons-learned.md, ai-led-session-workflow.md, and spec.md. User instructed: no external provider routing this session except cross-provider verification routed to Gemini Pro.

Wrote session-state.json marking session 4 of set 003 in-progress (currentSession=4, lifecycleState=work_in_progress, orchestrator=claude-code/claude-opus-4-7@high). Appended work_started event to session-events.jsonl.
Per durable user instruction (memory: feedback_ai_router_usage), routed ai-assignment.md authoring intentionally skipped this session.

Wired --manual-verify in close_session.py: added _prompt_manual_attestation helper for the interactive path; _validate_args now rejects --manual-verify without --interactive or --reason-file (silent-bypass blocker); run() collects attestation from reason file or interactive prompt, rejects empty/aborted input; closeout_requested event payload now carries manual_attestation when sourced from prompt vs reason when sourced from --reason-file; emits a single verification_completed event with method=manual, attestation=<text>, verdict=manual_attestation after the wait short-circuits (rides on existing event type per session 3 EVENT_TYPES-is-frozen rationale); run() signature takes optional prompt_fn for test injection. Updated existing test_manual_verify_skips_queue_wait to pass --reason-file (new validation rule).

Replaced _run_repair skeleton with real drift walk in close_session.py. Cross-checks session-events.jsonl, session-state.json, disposition.json, and queue databases. Four drift cases: (1) state-says-closed-but-no-closeout-event (bootstrapping legacy path) — auto-fixed with --apply by appending synthetic closeout_requested + closeout_succeeded carrying repaired=true and repair_reason=state_says_closed_but_no_closeout_event; (2) closeout_succeeded-in-events-but-state-not-flipped — auto-fixed by calling mark_session_complete; (3) stranded mid-closeout (closeout_requested without terminal companion) — reported only, recovery is reconciler's job; (4) disposition references missing queue messages — reported only, refuses to fabricate verdicts. Idempotent under repeat invocation. Uses _discover_queue_providers/_lookup_message helpers from the wait code; never modifies git state.

Wrote ai-router/tests/test_close_session_session4.py with 14 tests in 3 groups. Group 1 (manual-verify): attestation event lands with method=manual + attestation text + verdict=manual_attestation; interactive prompt path captures attestation onto closeout_requested as manual_attestation; missing-source rejected; empty/aborted input rejected. Group 2 (repair): no-drift clean session; state-says-closed-but-no-event diagnostic+apply+idempotent; event-says-closed-but-state-lagging diagnostic+apply; stranded mid-closeout reported but not auto-fixed; missing-queue-messages reported and refuses to synthesize verdicts; --apply-without-repair rejected end-to-end. Group 3 (4 e2e scenarios from Set 3 acceptance): outsource-first happy path; outsource-last happy path with verifier completing during wait; bootstrapping recovery via --repair --apply; manual-verify skips queue and gate runs and session closes. Also added 3 skeleton-level validation tests (manual_verify_without_attestation_source, manual_verify_with_interactive_passes, manual_verify_with_reason_file_passes).
Full pytest: 454 passed in ~45s on Windows (was 437 in Session 3 baseline; +17 net new tests: 14 session4 + 3 skeleton).

Routed end-of-session verification to gemini-pro per user instruction (overrode session-verification task_type_override which defaults to gpt-5-4; pinned max_tier=2 per lessons-learned to block auto-escalation). Verdict: VERIFIED on round 1 with no issues across all 5 evaluation areas. Cost $0.0502, in=34444t out=712t. Saved to session-reviews/session-004.md.

Authored docs/session-sets/003-closeout-script-and-deterministic-machinery/change-log.md directly per user instruction this session (no external provider routing except cross-provider verification). Documents all four sessions deliverables, test counts (374/415/437/454), all six acceptance criteria checked, risks-and-mitigations, and Set 4 follow-ups.
Marked session 4 complete in session-state.json with verdict=VERIFIED. Set 003 lifecycleState now closed. Emitted closeout_requested + closeout_succeeded events.
